V-M-003.checks: Provisioning and config generation remain compatible with route-aware topology. QR/config endpoints remain available to verified users with pending trial access before countdown activation. First VPN handshake can start the Phase-45 trial countdown without changing config rendering.
V-M-003.commands: pass-local 2026-06-05: Phase-73 preserved VPN config payloads with node scripts/phase70-config-qr-parity-smoke.mjs and node scripts/phase73-config-amnezia-qr-smoke.mjs; backend VPN/router API shape was unchanged.

scenarios.scenario: Provisioning selects correct topology and routing policy
Config rendered successfully for provisioned client
Pending trial user can retrieve first VPN config before countdown starts

V-M-003.evidence: 2026-06-01: Phase-45 backend suite passed with tests/test_vpn_router_compat.py and tests/test_trial_activation.py.

phase-70-verification-design.scope: VPN configuration payload and QR parity verification.
phase-70-verification-design.gate-1: Frontend QR, backend QR endpoint, and deploy/create-client generated QR must encode the same WireGuard/AWG config text for the same client fixture.
phase-70-verification-design.gate-2: QR readability changes must be limited to rendering parameters such as size, margin, or error-correction level after payload parity is proven.
phase-70-verification-design.gate-3: WireGuard/AWG profile fields, keys, endpoints, DNS, AllowedIPs, and interface/peer sections must not change during Phase-70.
phase-70-verification-design.gate-4: Decoded QR roundtrip must match the downloadable .conf payload exactly after normalizing line endings only.
phase-70-verification-design.commands: cd backend && pytest tests/test_vpn_service.py tests/test_vpn_router_compat.py tests/test_manual_client_cli.py; node scripts/phase70-config-qr-parity-smoke.mjs
phase-70-verification-design.blocked: Phase-70 must not edit deploy/create-client.sh, VPN server runtime, or generated client semantics unless a separate confirmed phase is created.

phase-70-evidence.command-1: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_vpn_router_compat.py tests/test_vpn_service.py tests/test_manual_client_cli.py -v
phase-70-evidence.summary: API config, download bytes, and backend QR payload source match exactly; QR rendering uses level M without changing config fields, keys, endpoint, DNS, AllowedIPs, or AWG profile content.

phase-73-verification-design.scope: VPN configuration payload and QR truthfulness verification for AmneziaWG versus AmneziaVPN UX.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-1: Working AmneziaWG QR must continue to encode the exact selected-device VPN config payload; Phase-73 may not mutate config text, keys, endpoint, DNS, AllowedIPs, profile flags, or preshared-key content.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-2: AmneziaVPN must not be presented as QR-scan compatible unless deterministic evidence proves the generated payload imports there; otherwise UI must route AmneziaVPN users to .conf download/import.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-3: QR rendering changes remain visual/UX-only and must keep Phase-70 config/download/QR parity green.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-4: Backend VPN/router tests are required if Phase-73 changes any config, download, QR, or device endpoint response shape.
phase-73-verification-design.commands: node scripts/phase70-config-qr-parity-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase73-config-amnezia-qr-smoke.mjs; backend vpn/router tests if API shape changes.

phase-73-evidence.summary: Phase-73 did not mutate VPN config text, QR payload source, keys, endpoint, DNS, AllowedIPs, or preshared-key content; AmneziaVPN QR is no longer advertised without proof.
